qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated memory exhaustion via REST provider unbounded request body read
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is in Traefik but targets the REST provider PUT handler reading request bodies with ioutil.ReadAll; it does not identify StripPrefixRegex, ForwardAuth/BasicAuth/DigestAuth interaction, RawPath slicing with a decoded prefix length, or dot-segment auth bypass.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described StripPrefixRegex path/RawPath desynchronization root cause or the resulting auth middleware bypass chain.
evidence: The advisory describes Traefik StripPrefixRegex matching a decoded URL path but slicing percent-encoded RawPath with len(prefix), producing paths such as /./admin/secret that bypass ForwardAuth, BasicAuth, or DigestAuth checks before backend dot-segment normalization.

The rest-provider-unbounded-memory finding describes provider/rest/rest.go reading an unauthenticated PUT body with ioutil.ReadAll and causing memory exhaustion, which is a separate Traefik component and root cause from the advisory.
